`importlib_resources` is a backport of Python standard library
[importlib.resources](https://docs.python.org/3/library/importlib.html#module-importlib.resources)
module for older Pythons.

The key goal of this module is to replace parts of [pkg_resources](https://setuptools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/pkg_resources.html) with a
solution in Python's stdlib that relies on well-defined APIs.  This makes
reading resources included in packages easier, with more stable and consistent
semantics.

# Compatibility

New features are introduced in this third-party library and later merged
into CPython. The following table indicates which versions of this library
were contributed to different versions in the standard library:

```{eval-rst}
.. list-table::
   :header-rows: 1

   * - importlib_resources
     - stdlib
   * - 5.8
     - 3.12
   * - 5.7
     - 3.11
   * - 5.0
     - 3.10
   * - 1.3
     - 3.9
   * - 0.5 (?)
     - 3.7
```

# For Enterprise

This project and the maintainers of thousands of other packages are working with Tidelift to deliver one enterprise subscription that covers all of the open source you use.

[Learn more](https://tidelift.com/subscription/pkg/pypi-importlib-resources?utm_source=pypi-importlib-resources&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=github).

# Security Contact

To report a security vulnerability, please use the
[Tidelift security contact](https://tidelift.com/security).
Tidelift will coordinate the fix and disclosure.

classes.name: importlib_resources.tests._compat.import_helper
documentation: Python 3.9 and earlier

classes.name: importlib_resources.tests._compat.os_helper

modules.name: importlib_resources.tests.update-zips
documentation:
Generate the zip test data files.

Run to build the tests/zipdataNN/ziptestdata.zip files from
files in tests/dataNN.

Replaces the file with the working copy, but does commit anything
to the source repo.

functions.name: importlib_resources.tests.update-zips.main
documentation:
>>> from unittest import mock
>>> monkeypatch = getfixture('monkeypatch')
>>> monkeypatch.setattr(zipfile, 'ZipFile', mock.MagicMock())
>>> print(); main()  # print workaround for bpo-32509
<BLANKLINE>
...data01... -> ziptestdata/...
...
...data02... -> ziptestdata/...
...

functions.name: importlib_resources.tests._path.build
documentation: from jaraco.path 3.4

modules.name: importlib_resources
documentation: Read resources contained within a package.

modules.name: importlib_resources.simple
documentation: Interface adapters for low-level readers.

classes.name: importlib_resources.simple.SimpleReader

methods.name: importlib_resources.simple.SimpleReader.package
documentation: The name of the package for which this reader loads resources.

methods.name: importlib_resources.simple.SimpleReader.children
documentation:
Obtain an iterable of SimpleReader for available
child containers (e.g. directories).

methods.name: importlib_resources.simple.SimpleReader.resources
documentation: Obtain available named resources for this virtual package.

methods.name: importlib_resources.simple.SimpleReader.open_binary
documentation: Obtain a File-like for a named resource.

The minimum, low-level interface required from a resource
provider.

classes.name: importlib_resources.simple.ResourceContainer
documentation: Traversable container for a package's resources via its reader.

classes.name: importlib_resources.simple.ResourceHandle
documentation: Handle to a named resource in a ResourceReader.

classes.name: importlib_resources.simple.TraversableReader
documentation:
A TraversableResources based on SimpleReader. Resource providers
may derive from this class to provide the TraversableResources
interface by supplying the SimpleReader interface.

methods.name: importlib_resources.tests.test_path.PathDiskTests.test_natural_path
documentation:
Guarantee the internal implementation detail that
file-system-backed resources do not get the tempdir
treatment.

methods.name: importlib_resources.readers.FileReader.resource_path
documentation:
Return the file system path to prevent
`resources.path()` from creating a temporary
copy.

classes.name: importlib_resources.readers.MultiplexedPath
documentation:
Given a series of Traversable objects, implement a merged
version of the interface across all objects. Useful for
namespace packages which may be multihomed at a single
name.

methods.name: importlib_resources.readers.NamespaceReader.resource_path
documentation:
Return the file system path to prevent
`resources.path()` from creating a temporary
copy.

methods.name: importlib_resources.tests.test_files.FilesTests.test_old_parameter
documentation:
Files used to take a 'package' parameter. Make sure anyone
passing by name is still supported.

methods.name: importlib_resources.tests.test_files.ModulesFilesTests.test_module_resources
documentation: A module can have resources found adjacent to the module.

methods.name: importlib_resources.tests.test_files.ImplicitContextFilesTests.test_implicit_files
documentation: Without any parameter, files() will infer the location as the caller.

classes.name: importlib_resources.abc.ResourceReader

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.ResourceReader.open_resource
documentation:
Return an opened, file-like object for binary reading.

The 'resource' argument is expected to represent only a file name.
If the resource cannot be found, FileNotFoundError is raised.

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.ResourceReader.resource_path
documentation:
Return the file system path to the specified resource.

The 'resource' argument is expected to represent only a file name.
If the resource does not exist on the file system, raise
FileNotFoundError.

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.ResourceReader.is_resource
documentation:
Return True if the named 'path' is a resource.

Files are resources, directories are not.

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.ResourceReader.contents
documentation: Return an iterable of entries in `package`.
Abstract base class for loaders to provide resource reading support.

classes.name: importlib_resources.abc.Traversable

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.Traversable.iterdir
documentation: Yield Traversable objects in self

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.Traversable.read_bytes
documentation: Read contents of self as bytes

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.Traversable.read_text
documentation: Read contents of self as text

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.Traversable.is_dir
documentation: Return True if self is a directory

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.Traversable.is_file
documentation: Return True if self is a file

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.Traversable.joinpath
documentation:
Return Traversable resolved with any descendants applied.

Each descendant should be a path segment relative to self
and each may contain multiple levels separated by
`posixpath.sep` (`/`).

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.Traversable.__truediv__
documentation: Return Traversable child in self

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.Traversable.open
documentation:
mode may be 'r' or 'rb' to open as text or binary. Return a handle
suitable for reading (same as pathlib.Path.open).

When opening as text, accepts encoding parameters such as those
accepted by io.TextIOWrapper.

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.Traversable.name
documentation: The base name of this object without any parent references.

An object with a subset of pathlib.Path methods suitable for
traversing directories and opening files.

Any exceptions that occur when accessing the backing resource
may propagate unaltered.

classes.name: importlib_resources.abc.TraversableResources

methods.name: importlib_resources.abc.TraversableResources.files
documentation: Return a Traversable object for the loaded package.

The required interface for providing traversable
resources.

functions.name: importlib_resources._legacy.normalize_path
documentation:
Normalize a path by ensuring it is a string.

If the resulting string contains path separators, an exception is raised.

functions.name: importlib_resources._legacy.open_binary
documentation: Return a file-like object opened for binary reading of the resource.

functions.name: importlib_resources._legacy.read_binary
documentation: Return the binary contents of the resource.

functions.name: importlib_resources._legacy.open_text
documentation: Return a file-like object opened for text reading of the resource.

functions.name: importlib_resources._legacy.read_text
documentation:
Return the decoded string of the resource.

The decoding-related arguments have the same semantics as those of
bytes.decode().

functions.name: importlib_resources._legacy.contents
documentation:
Return an iterable of entries in `package`.

Note that not all entries are resources.  Specifically, directories are
not considered resources.  Use `is_resource()` on each entry returned here
to check if it is a resource or not.

functions.name: importlib_resources._legacy.is_resource
documentation:
True if `name` is a resource inside `package`.

Directories are *not* resources.

functions.name: importlib_resources._legacy.path
documentation:
A context manager providing a file path object to the resource.

If the resource does not already exist on its own on the file system,
a temporary file will be created. If the file was created, the file
will be deleted upon exiting the context manager (no exception is
raised if the file was deleted prior to the context manager
exiting).

modules.name: importlib_resources._compat
documentation: flake8: noqa

functions.name: importlib_resources._compat.wrap_spec
documentation:
Construct a package spec with traversable compatibility
on the spec/loader/reader.

Supersedes _adapters.wrap_spec to use TraversableResourcesLoader
from above for older Python compatibility (<3.10).

classes.name: importlib_resources._compat.TraversableResourcesLoader
documentation:
Adapt loaders to provide TraversableResources and other
compatibility.

Used primarily for Python 3.9 and earlier where the native
loaders do not yet implement TraversableResources.

classes.name: importlib_resources.tests.util.CommonTests

methods.name: importlib_resources.tests.util.CommonTests.execute
documentation:
Call the pertinent legacy API function (e.g. open_text, path)
on package and path.
Tests shared by test_open, test_path, and test_read.

functions.name: importlib_resources._common.package_to_anchor
documentation:
Replace 'package' parameter as 'anchor' and warn about the change.

Other errors should fall through.

>>> files('a', 'b')
Traceback (most recent call last):
TypeError: files() takes from 0 to 1 positional arguments but 2 were given

functions.name: importlib_resources._common.files
documentation: Get a Traversable resource for an anchor.

functions.name: importlib_resources._common.get_resource_reader
documentation: Return the package's loader if it's a ResourceReader.

functions.name: importlib_resources._common._infer_caller
documentation: Walk the stack and find the frame of the first caller not in this module.

functions.name: importlib_resources._common.from_package
documentation: Return a Traversable object for the given package.

functions.name: importlib_resources._common._is_present_dir
documentation:
Some Traversables implement `is_dir()` to raise an
exception (i.e. `FileNotFoundError`) when the
directory doesn't exist. This function wraps that call
to always return a boolean and only return True
if there's a dir and it exists.

functions.name: importlib_resources._common.as_file
documentation:
Given a Traversable object, return that object as a
path on the local file system in a context manager.

functions.name: importlib_resources._common._
documentation: Degenerate behavior for pathlib.Path objects.

functions.name: importlib_resources._common._temp_path
documentation: Wrap tempfile.TemporyDirectory to return a pathlib object.

functions.name: importlib_resources._common._temp_dir
documentation:
Given a traversable dir, recursively replicate the whole tree
to the file system in a context manager.

functions.name: importlib_resources._adapters.wrap_spec
documentation:
Construct a package spec with traversable compatibility
on the spec/loader/reader.

classes.name: importlib_resources._adapters.SpecLoaderAdapter
documentation: Adapt a package spec to adapt the underlying loader.

classes.name: importlib_resources._adapters.TraversableResourcesLoader
documentation: Adapt a loader to provide TraversableResources.

classes.name: importlib_resources._adapters.CompatibilityFiles

methods.name: importlib_resources._adapters.CompatibilityFiles._native
documentation: Return the native reader if it supports files().

inner_classes.name: importlib_resources._adapters.CompatibilityFiles.SpecPath
documentation:
Path tied to a module spec.
Can be read and exposes the resource reader children.

inner_classes.name: importlib_resources._adapters.CompatibilityFiles.ChildPath
documentation:
Path tied to a resource reader child.
Can be read but doesn't expose any meaningful children.

inner_classes.name: importlib_resources._adapters.CompatibilityFiles.OrphanPath
documentation:
Orphan path, not tied to a module spec or resource reader.
Can't be read and doesn't expose any meaningful children.

Adapter for an existing or non-existent resource reader
to provide a compatibility .files().

methods.name: importlib_resources.tests.test_resource.ResourceTests.test_is_file_exists
documentation: Subclasses are expected to set the `data` attribute.

methods.name: importlib_resources.tests.test_resource.ResourceFromZipsTest02.test_unrelated_contents
documentation:
Test thata zip with two unrelated subpackages return
distinct resources. Ref python/importlib_resources#44.

classes.name: importlib_resources.tests.test_resource.DeletingZipsTest
documentation:
Having accessed resources in a zip file should not keep an open
reference to the zip.
